mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits an easier discount-rate regime to equities, lifting valuation multiples and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because rate cuts without renewed price pressure reduce real financing costs.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or not above baseline after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal disappears when CPIAUCSL is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level marks weakening household income and weaker aggregate demand, which compresses earnings expectations and reduces S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be visible when labor-market deterioration is broad enough to affect PAYEMS growth as well.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment rises are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if PAYEMS does not deteriorate in the same windows.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should lift short-horizon equity returns through higher sales expectations and improved operating leverage. The effect should be strongest when industrial output is also firm, confirming that demand is not being offset by a supply-side slowdown.
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS does not precede positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign is reversed in periods when INDPRO is stable or rising.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO with low HOUST weakness signals a clean cyclical rebound in physical production rather than a demand slump, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when industrial gains are accompanied by weakening labor data, indicating output is not broad-based.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in INDPRO and HOUST do not align with positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes when UNRATE is rising.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated FEDFUNDS rate above the neutral-looking range tightens financial conditions and lowers equity valuations by raising the discount rate on future cash flows. This bearish effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is still elevated, because sticky inflation delays policy relief and keeps real rates restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS episodes are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect does not strengthen when CPIAUCSL remains high.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent PAYEMS decline signals payroll contraction and weaker household spending power, which should pressure S&P 500 returns within a month through lower revenue expectations and risk-off positioning. The signal is weaker when UNRATE is flat, because payroll losses without higher unemployment are less informative about aggregate demand.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month payroll declines fail to precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect persists even when UNRATE is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: A housing rebound, captured by rising HOUST, improves cyclical growth expectations and supports equities because residential investment is an early-cycle demand lever. The effect should appear over one quarter and be strongest when RSAFS is also improving, confirming that housing strength is part of a broader demand upswing.
failure_condition: Reject if housing upswings do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is absent when retail sales are simultaneously improving.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates improving real output without reacceleration in inflation, a combination that lowers recession fears and supports positive equity returns over the next month. The mechanism fails when production gains come with rising inflation, because cost pressure offsets the growth signal.
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO gains and CPI stability do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or if returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL is accelerating.
